title: senecio jacobaea var. pinnatifidus
intro: Senecio jacobaea var. pinnatifidus, also known as ragwort or Jacob's staff, is a biennial plant native to Europe and western Asia, characterized by its distinctive pinnatifid leaves and yellow daisy-like flowers. The plant contains pyrrolizidine alkaloids, which have been traditionally used for their medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antispasmodic, and expectorant effects. In herbal medicine, Senecio jacobaea var. pinnatifidus has been used to treat a range of health issues, including respiratory problems, such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as digestive issues, like constipation and irritable bowel syndrome. Senecio jacobaea var. pinnatifidus can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and is often combined with other herbs to enhance its therapeutic effects.
